schema_notes: Per-billionaire record following Ellison v3 template. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events canonically scoped by event_role. Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream.

person.wealth_source: Founder, chairman, and largest shareholder of Interactive Brokers Group (IBKR); pioneer of electronic securities trading. Concentrated position in IBKR common stock (publicly traded).

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 list (March 2026): $82.9B, ranked #23 worldwide and #1 in Palm Beach. Up from $57.3B on the 2025 list — a ~45% increase driven by IBKR stock appreciation. Bloomberg Billionaires Index and gurufocus report $73.3B–$89.2B in real-time tracking. Wikipedia still cites the older $57.3B figure (Nov 2025).

sources.publisher: Forbes 2026 (via Boca Raton Tribune / Palm Beach Daily News analysis)

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Peterffy's ~75% stake in Interactive Brokers is public equity, so nominally liquid, but divesting would move the stock and is Rule 144-restricted. Applying ~35% liquidity discount for 'giving capacity now' — more liquid than Ellison (25%) because IBKR is less concentrated in his identity than Oracle is in Ellison's. No disclosed family-office side pocket at the scale of Lawrence Investments.

cited_events.recipient: Various (74+ recipients; environmental and Indigenous-focused NGOs)

cited_events.dedup_note: CRITICAL: This is a transfer INTO the Peterffy Foundation (in shares), NOT out-the-door giving. Per dedup rule #2, we count grants OUT (captured in peterffy_pf_2020 through peterffy_pf_2024), not transfers in. Explains why 2020+ grants-out jumped ~20x vs. 2019. Classified as transfer_in and excluded from observable rollup.

cited_events.notes: Peterffy is Chairman & CEO (zero comp). Classification unclear — Inside Philanthropy describes it as a 'Balance for Freedom' vehicle; NTEE categorization is 'Corporate Foundations.' Activity collapsed after 2018. Possible the operating mission shifted or was wound down de facto; no 2025 data yet.

cited_events.recipient: Vanderbilt University — Financial Markets Research Center (renamed Hans Stoll Financial Markets Research Center in 2021)
cited_events.amount_note: Reported as a $3M equity stake in Interactive Brokers at time of gift. Pre-IPO IBKR value; actual realized value to Vanderbilt upon any liquidation would differ. Treating face value as reported.
cited_events.dedup_note: Pre-dates Peterffy Foundation (2014) and Balance for Freedom (2016) — cannot be duplicated against either. Standalone direct personal gift.

pledges_and_announcements.notes: Peterffy has not signed the Giving Pledge. Verified: his name does not appear on the Giving Pledge pledger list. Inside Philanthropy (2025): 'if he has a second life as a major charitable donor, he has kept it tightly under wraps.' No public successor commitment of comparable magnitude identified.

pledges_and_announcements.award: Grand Cross of the Hungarian Order of Merit
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Recognition, not a donation. No publicly documented major philanthropic gift to Hungarian causes identified. Peterffy's primary Hungary-related 'giving back' appears to be locating Interactive Brokers' Central European operations in Budapest (2021) — a for-profit decision, not charity. Inside Philanthropy notes one Peterffy Foundation grant to Order of Malta American Association to support Hungarian language/culture revitalization among at-risk youth in Transylvania, Romania — amount not disclosed separately from foundation totals.

pledges_and_announcements.award: Lead Medalist, 41st Annual American Free Enterprise Day
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Recognition event; no publicly disclosed accompanying gift to PBA. Peterffy's address reportedly centered on his immigration story, not philanthropy commitments.

pledges_and_announcements.recipient: Palm Beach police/firefighter scholarship auction (Mar-a-Lago charity event)
pledges_and_announcements.amount_note: Peterffy donated round-trip use of his private jet as part of an auction package that sold for $2M (winning bidder: Herbert Wertheim). Peterffy did NOT pay $2M — he contributed the flight. Value of the jet time unquantified in reporting.
pledges_and_announcements.dedup_note: Correcting common misreporting — some secondary sources imply Peterffy paid $2M for a Mar-a-Lago lunch. The $2M was paid by Wertheim; Peterffy only donated the jet. Excluded from observable giving as amount is not quantified and benefit was a local scholarship fund, not a research or programmatic grant.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1.
political_giving.observed_total_note: OpenSecrets-sourced floor of $13M to GOP super PACs in 2020+2022 cycles alone. Additional $500K + $344,660 to Trump 47 Committee (Aug 2024), $3M+ to Youngkin's Spirit of Virginia PAC (three $1M tranches), plus $100K pro-Trump 2016 and $60K+ RNC 2011. True total likely $20M+ but FEC bulk-pull deferred. Peterffy self-described December 2024: 'I've always been a Republican all my life.'

sources.note: Peterffy withdrew support from DeSantis after 6-week abortion ban — evidence of active partisan engagement, not passive check-writing.

foundations_active.name: The Peterffy Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP (Alexandra, Catherine, William Peterffy — Thomas's three children — plus family lawyer Christopher Uzpen. Thomas Peterffy himself is NOT on the board.)
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: Grants out 2024 ($7.3M) / assets ($172M) = 4.2% — BELOW the 5% legal minimum for private foundations. Same pattern 2022–2024 (4.2%–4.7%). Potential LOW_PAYOUT flag but technically foundations average across years and can carry forward, so not yet a violation.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT_VERIFIED — grant recipients include environmental NGOs (Stand.earth, Canopy via Tides, Amazon Watch, Land is Life, Colectivo Quipa, NDN Collective). Tides Foundation grant is a DAF/fiscal-sponsor intermediary but amount is small ($300K) and recipient purpose (Canopy) is named.
foundations_active.thematic_focus: Environmental conservation, Indigenous rights, environmental justice, reproductive health
foundations_active.note: Family-controlled. Thomas Peterffy funds it but does not direct grantmaking. Inside Philanthropy: foundation diverges ideologically from the patriarch's Republican politics — e.g., $20K to Planned Parenthood in 2024.

foundations_active.name: Balance for Freedom Inc
foundations_active.officers: Thomas Peterffy (Chairman & CEO), Earl Nemser, Christopher Uzpen
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: Near-zero grants in 5 of last 6 years: 2019 $782, 2020 $510, 2021 $0, 2022 $954, 2023 $1,226, 2024 $201K. Earlier years (2015-2018) grants totaled $9.3M. Cumulative activity $9.47M. With $13.7M in current assets and $1,226 in 2023 grants, the effective payout rate is ~0.01% — a DORMANT_FOUNDATION flag. The 5% minimum distribution requirement may be at risk.
foundations_active.note: Only vehicle where Peterffy himself sits as officer. Purpose per Inside Philanthropy is ideological/'freedom' — but grantmaking has effectively stopped. Flag: possible WAREHOUSING structure.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No direct DAF activity identified. Peterffy Foundation routes ~$300K/yr through Tides Foundation (a DAF-adjacent intermediary) for Canopy; de minimis at the Peterffy scale.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: No philanthropic LLC identified (no CZI / Emerson Collective / Ballmer Group analog).
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2.
detected_vehicles.trusts_note: No publicly disclosed charitable trust structure. Absence of disclosure is itself informative: unlike Ellison (who disclosed a trust in his Giving Pledge letter), Peterffy has made no public commitment about estate disposition. Default prior: majority passes to three children, who already control the $172M Peterffy Foundation.

red_flags.evidence: Net worth $82.9B; cumulative observable lifetime giving (Peterffy Foundation $36.5M since 2015 + Balance for Freedom $9.5M since 2015 + Vanderbilt $3M in 2003) totals ~$49M. This is 0.06% of current net worth and ~0.2% of 10-year liquidity-adjusted expected giving. Inside Philanthropy (2025): 'if he has a second life as a major charitable donor, he has kept it tightly under wraps.'

$13M+ to GOP super PACs in 2020+2022 alone, vs. $7M/yr total foundation giving. Known political giving 2011–2024 is ~$20M, at least on par with and likely exceeding cumulative charitable giving. NBER substitution coefficient applies.

Balance for Freedom Inc (EIN 81-0899116) holds $13.7M in assets but made zero or near-zero grants in 5 of the last 6 years (2019: $782; 2020: $510; 2021: $0; 2022: $954; 2023: $1,226). Peterffy is the only Peterffy family member on the board. Pattern resembles a warehousing/holding structure rather than an active grantmaker.

Peterffy has not signed the Giving Pledge. Unlike many peer billionaires, he has made no public commitment about estate disposition. Combined with $82.9B net worth, age 81, and three adult children who control the active family foundation, default prior is dynastic wealth transfer with a $172M foundation as the 'philanthropy' carve-out (~0.2% of fortune).
Forbes 2025 to Forbes 2026: $57.3B → $82.9B (+45% in ~12 months). Peterffy Foundation 2023→2024 grants-out: $8.13M → $7.3M (DECREASED 10%). Wealth growth is not reflected in giving trajectory.

Peterffy Foundation 2024: $7.3M grants / $172M assets = 4.2%. Below 5% minimum distribution requirement. 2022: $8.2M / assets (~$155M est) = 5.3%; 2023: $8.1M / $178M (peak) = 4.6%. Trending down. IRS allows 5-year averaging so not an automatic violation, but a yellow flag.

red_flags_not_present: HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (all officers of both foundations zero-comp)
OFFSHORE_ENTITIES (not yet checked — cannot claim absent)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(Peterffy Foundation 990-PF grants_out 2015-2024 = $36.48M) + sum(Balance for Freedom Inc grants_out 2015-2024 = $9.47M) + Vanderbilt 2003 direct_gift ($3M, pre-foundation). Excludes: $78M transfer INTO Peterffy Foundation in 2020; political giving; unquantified 2026 jet donation; Hungarian Order of Merit (award only).
observable_giving_todo: Pull SEC EDGAR Form 4 G-transactions for Peterffy's IBKR CIK — any stock gifts to 501(c)(3) not captured via foundation filings.
FEC bulk pull to refine political total (floor: $20M+).
Brave Search for post-2024 announced gifts (pre-2015 period may contain additional Vanderbilt-style direct gifts).
Verify whether Vanderbilt 2003 IBKR equity gift has been included in or is separate from subsequent filings.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.35 × $82.9B = $29.02B; tenure cap saturated (~20 years). Expected = $2.90B. Strict denominator matching the formal 10% norm.

hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked — zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously sometimes. Peterffy profile (private, Republican, disfavors public attention) raises the prior that some giving is off-books. Apply conservative upper bound of 0.5% of liquid wealth: 0.005 × $82.9B × 0.35 = $145M. Rounded to $150M as upper.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious affiliation with tithing norms.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: Unlike Ellison, Peterffy has NOT disclosed any charitable trust. No public commitment language to credit. Zero uplift — cannot assign probability to undeclared estate intentions.
hidden_upper_usd.hungarian_giving_method: Hungarian Order of Merit (2017) and IBKR-Budapest locating decision suggest homeland ties. No major public Hungarian philanthropic gift identified. Apply modest $10M upper bound for plausible off-shore Hungarian giving that would not appear in US 990 filings (e.g., direct gifts to Hungarian foundations, cultural institutions, Order of Malta work in Transylvania).

rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_LOW requires (observable + hidden_upper) / expected < 0.2. Peterffy: ($49M + $160M) / $2.90B = 7.2%, well below 20% threshold. Unlike Ellison — who has a disclosed trust that introduces outcome uncertainty — Peterffy has made no comparable public commitment. He qualifies for the strictest tier classification.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Peterffy is a structural example of the 'private billionaire' archetype: $82.9B fortune, public political engagement ($20M+ GOP), minimal public philanthropy ($49M lifetime observable), no Giving Pledge, no disclosed charitable trust. The Peterffy Foundation is a family vehicle run by his children ($172M assets, $7M/yr out) — impressive in absolute terms, negligible relative to his net worth (0.2% of fortune). Balance for Freedom Inc is effectively dormant. Default prior absent new disclosure: primarily dynastic wealth.

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: v2-style computation for pipeline continuity. No pledge penalty because he never signed (can't breach what you didn't sign). Full red-flag penalty (6 flags capped at 20). Replaced 'pledge_breach' weight with 'low_profile_bonus' reflecting the 49M / 2.9B expected gap. DO NOT PUBLISH per PLAN.md.

right_of_reply.planned_outreach_route: Interactive Brokers Investor Relations (publicly listed) + The Peterffy Foundation (West Palm Beach, FL, via filing address) + Balance for Freedom Inc (via Christopher Uzpen, family lawyer listed on both filings).

VERIFY: FEC bulk pull to get exact 2011-2024 political total (floor $20M; likely higher).
VERIFY: Pull pre-2015 Peterffy charitable history (he was a billionaire from ~2005 — decade of activity not yet captured).
